channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to know about Jared Birchall, Elon Musk’s right-hand man
item.description: A Wall Street Journal report this week gave an extensive look into how Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and a senior Trump adviser, handles his companies and his more than a dozen children. The answer, it seems, is by relying heavily on his longtime right-hand man, Jared Birchall.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix beats first quarter forecast, revealing it hasn’t been touched by Trump’s tariffs, yet
item.description: Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: TSA tests out AI to train, assist airport screening officers
item.description: TSA is testing the use of AI and virtual reality to help assist its tens of thousands of airport screeners The post TSA tests out AI to train, assist airport screening officers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump administration replaces federal COVID-19 response page with 'lab leak' explainer
item.description: The Trump White House on Friday replaced the covid.gov website that provided resources and information on COVID-19 with a webpage promoting the theory that the pandemic was a result of a laboratory leak in Wuhan, China, which a House GOP panel strongly favored in a report released last year.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Everything leaving Hulu in May 2025
item.description: Hulu has announced all the titles leaving the platform in May 2025 – and there's a surprising amount of documentaries in here.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Google previews Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model
item.description: Google has introduced an early preview of its Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model. An early version of the model is available through the Gemini API via Google AI Studio and Vertex AI, according to an April 17 Google blog post.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Entertainment venue management giant Legends International reveals major data breach
item.description: Company lost sensitive data in the November 2024 breach, and is now notifying potentially affected individuals.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Judge Rules Google Violated Antitrust Laws: Implications for Tech Giant
item.description: A US judge has ruled Google violated antitrust laws by monopolizing the ad market, potentially leading to significant consequences for the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple and Google eye the future of AI glasses
item.description: Apple and Google know that smart glasses will replace the smartphone as the main device people use the most, according to recent news. And the two leading smartphone platform makers don’t want Meta to own the future of mobile computing, but they know Meta is leading the race so far.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tiny startup could challenge Wasabi, iDrive, and BackBlaze with sovereign EU cloud storage solution at rock-bottom prices
item.description: Estonia’s Storadera offers a low-cost, sovereign alternative to US cloud providers, aiming to disrupt the storage market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Roblox issued subpoena for potentially exposing children to graphic, harmful content
item.description: The attorney general expressed concern over the ability of predatory adults to freely send messages to minors.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Broadcom (quietly) brings back free VMware ESXi hypervisor
item.description: With little notice or fanfare, Broadcom is offering a free version of the VMware ESXi hypervisor for download. VMware vSphere Hypervisor version 8 can be downloaded from Broadcom’s support portal for free, but registration is required.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: You need to update your Apple devices immediately
item.description: Things are getting serious regarding Apple security. This shouldn’t really surprise you, given the normalization of chaos we’re enduring, as confusion always makes people more vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Users receive unprovoked Windows 11 offers after Intune code glitch
item.description: Enterprise users are receiving unprovoked offers to upgrade to Windows 11 due to an issue in Microsoft’s device management tools. The issue is with Microsoft’s Intune software, which typically allows system administrators to manage mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts
item.description: Recent advances in AI are revolutionizing legal due diligence by enhancing efficiency and accuracy. The post 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Zuckerberg underscores TikTok competition as Meta fights monopoly allegations
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized Wednesday that his social media company faces stiff competition from TikTok, as Meta seeks to fend off accusations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that it has a monopoly over social networking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia expects ban on chip exports to China to cost $5.5B
item.description: Nvidia now expects new US government restrictions on exports of its H20 chip to China will cost the company as much as $5.5 billion, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel sells off majority stake in its FPGA business
item.description: Intel has spun off its programmable solutions group as a standalone FPGA company, selling a majority stake in the company to a private equity firm. Intel is taking a fairly hefty loss on this deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A colossal squid is caught on camera for the first time in the deep sea
item.description: A colossal squid has been captured on camera for the first time in the deep sea by an international team of researchers steering a remotely operated submersible.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CVE funding cuts reversed after security concerns raised
item.description: Editor’s note: After this story was published earlier today, CISA signed a contract extension that averts a shutdown of the MITRE CVE program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Analysts: Go slow on M365, Google Workspace ‘agent-ish’ AI rollouts
item.description: The last few months have seen an explosion of new agentic-like AI tools that can automate workflows within productivity applications, but analysts are recommending that enterprises carefully assess deployment risks before adopting them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 languages you can deploy to WebAssembly right now
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, gives developers a way to create programs that run at near-native speed in the browser or anywhere else you can deploy the WebAssembly runtime. But you generally don’t write programs in Wasm directly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI GPT-4.1 models promise improved coding and instruction following
item.description: OpenAI has announced a new family of models, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and GPT-4.1 nano, which it says outperforms GPT-4o and GPT-4o mini “across the board.” In conjunction with the launch of the GPT-4.1 family, OpenAI also announced that it is deprecating GPT-4.5 Preview in the API.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation
